mission.title: dex: Possibility to select connector in client request
mission.description: Possibility to select connector in client request. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current dex deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dex version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
helm list -n dex 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dex installation.

steps.title: Review dex configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dex configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
kubectl get configmap -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dex
```
Right now if there is more than one connector configured user has to select one before he is redirected to the authentication page. It should be possible to skip that step if a client has preferred connector, e.g. google.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Possibility to select connector in client request
steps.description: Agree with above, this is not a good solution when utilizing open source clients that check for `. /well-known/openid-configuration` ... I modified the logs in dex to show this issue in practice when I try to pass the `connector_id` via query param from an open source client. ``` 400 Bad Request: ```yaml 400 Bad Request: dex

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
kubectl get events -n dex --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Possibility to select connector in client request" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Agree with  above, this is not a good solution when utilizing open source clients that check for `. /well-known/openid-configuration` ... I modified the logs in dex to show this issue in practice when I try to pass the `connector_id` via query param from an open source client.
codeSnippets:
400 Bad Request: dex

Bad Request

Connector ID does not match a valid Connector: okta-argo/.well-known/openid-configuration

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dex installed or the issue environment reproducible.
